response.justification: The candidate substantially exceeds the 6+ years requirement with roughly 15 years spanning frontend, backend, mobile, and embedded work, and his recent roles map directly onto the core stack: TypeScript/Node.js product work at Google, TypeScript/React/MongoDB/AWS at Meta, and Node/TypeScript/GraphQL with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) at RIDE. He also has explicit AI-agent development experience (MCP code generators and agentic-workflow tooling), which is a required skill many applicants lack, plus Docker/Kubernetes and a CS degree. Gaps are mostly framework-specific — no explicit Nest.js, Vitest, or Kotlin — and much of his frontend history is Ember rather than React, so React depth and Nest.js familiarity should be probed in interview.
strengths: Deep full-stack TypeScript/Node.js experience at scale, including recent Google and Meta roles
Hands-on AI-agent development (MCP code generators, agentic workflow frameworks) matching a required skill
Strong testing and engineering-practice pedigree: TDD, CI/CD, authored test frameworks, taught testing courses
concerns: No explicit Nest.js, Vitest, or Kotlin experience; Java exposure is dated
Frontend history leans heavily on Ember.js, with React evidenced mainly in one role
Resume inconsistencies (e.g. 'Founder, Amazon' describing an open-source NGO side project) warrant clarification
